description_long: This is a heavy-duty electric drill with a robust, industrial design, featuring a thick, ergonomic handle with a prominent trigger area and a visible power cord extending from the rear. The body is primarily composed of a yellow-orange plastic casing with black and gray accents, and the front section is made of metallic material, likely aluminum or steel, for durability and heat dissipation. The drill head is large and cylindrical, designed to accommodate substantial drill bits, and the side of the casing displays a label with technical information. Ventilation slots are present on the metallic section to prevent overheating during prolonged use. The overall appearance suggests it is built for demanding construction or demolition tasks, commonly found in urban repair or renovation settings.
description: A rugged, yellow-orange electric drill with a metallic front, ergonomic handle, and visible power cord, designed for heavy-duty urban construction tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the ergonomic handle, power cord, and technical label on the casing.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large cylindrical drill head and the alignment of the handle above it.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle, power cord, and additional details of the metallic front section.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end of the handle and the exit point of the power cord.
